programmes.show_description: Alf, Mrs Hollingbery and Arthur are preparing for their trip to Australia. S4 E4
With The Police. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
Simon investigates when an inventor's satellite guidance system falls into the wrong hands and his wife and daughter are used in an attempt to coerce him to defect. S2 E13: The Sporting Chance

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
Rudolf Hess flew to Scotland on a secret mission. Decades later he died in prison. Was it suicide or murder. Narrated by Robert Powell.
Part 2 of the documentary examining a team of archeologists who reveal a giant ring of prehistoric shafts just a few miles away from Stonehenge and the ancient henge monument of Durrington Walls. Ep2
...You? There are some news events that change the world - in this episode, we take you back to April 1986 and the Chernobyl disaster and its wider implications.
A look into the lives of the rich and famous; Beyonce rose to fame with Destiny's Child and then went on to forge a solo career as a singer and film star.
London is a huge city with a massive story to tell; we explore the London Underground, the world's first underground transport system with a logo that is recognised by people all over the world.
The F-4 Phantom II is an American tandem two-seat, twin-engine, long-range supersonic jet interceptor and fighter-bomber originally developed by McDonnell Aircraft for the United States Navy. S3 E5

The Gallipoli campaign was the land-based part of a strategy intended to allow Allied ships to pass through the Dardanelles, capture Constantinople, and ultimately knock Ottoman Turkey out of the war.
The story of the price many soldiers had to pay in order for the Allies to take France back from the Germans. Ep10: D-Day and Liberation
We explore some of the most infamous battles in America's fight against terror as the Vietnam war begins, we look at the activities and stories of Medal of Honor recipients and the end of the war.
Examining the history of war tactics. Flank attack - going around the side of the enemy - is a basic manoeuvre, yet stories of victories decided by flanking fill military textbooks. S2 E4
The 1920s and 1930s were uncertain times for the RAF and all other armed services; with peace came cuts in spending on new aircraft development. Ep2: Under Distant Skies
The story of the Blitz of Britain and how Nazi bombers guided to targets by electronic beams were defeated by Dr Reginald Jones in the Battle of the Beams. Ep6: The Man Who Saved Britain's Cities
After WWII, the incredible and innovative weapon systems created by the Third Reich - from jet engines to guided missiles - were uncovered. S1 E11: Secret Weapons of the Third Reich
This programme takes a look at the illustrious history of the Chindits, a group formed by Major General Orde Wingate to fight the Japanese in Burma. Ep11: The Chindits
Arthur announces a house move to Kent to Jessie and says she must accompany him. Sir Horatio refuses to pay Jack for taking part in his housing estate scheme. S3 E3: My Bonnie Lass, Goodbye
Arthur spots a way to make money out of his brother-in-law Brian's misery.
George pursues a vacant seat in Parliament as Agatha plans for her hundredth birthday, and Ross is given a proposition. Starring Aidan Turner. S3 E7
Drama series. Simon Neary is a gangster that Gene has wanted to nail for years, but is stifling his gun deal a way to keep Alex from getting shot in 2008? S1 E5
Alf and Mrs Hollingbery are off to Australia to meet her rich brother and to get married. Alf is not sure which he is more frightened of - flying or getting married. S4 E5
With BA Robertson and Mel Smith. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy show from Thames TV, all in the best possible taste!
Simon gets involved when Mrs Yarmouth is convinced to hand over church funds by husband-and-wife confidence tricksters. S2 E14: The Bunco Artists

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
Mata Hari became an icon of betrayal, accused of seducing secrets for espionage. But were the charges real, or was she a scapegoat in World War I hysteria? Narrated by Robert Powell.
Susan learns about the One O'Clock Gun in Edinburgh and the hiding place of Scotland's crown jewels at Edinburgh Castle before getting to grips with golf at St Andrews. S1 E1: Edinburgh
...Were You? On the 1st of September, 2004, terrorists took 1,100 people, including 777 children, hostage in the Beslan School in Russia - the ensuing massacre saw over 300 people killed.
Johnny Depp has become one of the most recognisable faces in Hollywood. We take a look at his career.
There would have been no London without the River Thames, and it grew firstly as a port and trading route; we go behind the scenes at Westminster, Chelsea, Greenwich and the Tower of London.
The Grumman TBF Avenger is an American WWII torpedo bomber developed initially for the United States Navy and Marine Corps and used by several air and naval aviation services around the world. S3 E6
The landing at Anzac Cove in April 1915 was part of the amphibious invasion of the Gallipoli Peninsula by the forces of the British Empire and began the land phase of the First World War.
Survivors recount the horrors behind surviving a merciless place and hear the shock of Allied soldiers as they discover the evil secret of Germany's concentration camps. Ep11: Fall of Berlin
The USA has been at the forefront of fighting acts of terror across the world. We look at the development of the Delta force and the use of Special Units in Iran, Grenada, Panama and the Gulf War.
Examining the history of war tactics. Special operations are the cutting edge of warfare. Undertaken by elite personnel, they can cover everything from intelligence gathering to raiding. S2 E5
The RAF had aircraft that could escort bombers to targets deep inside Germany and back by the end of World War One, and the Hawker Hunter and F-86 Sabre took the RAF supersonic. Ep3: A Vicious Sting
The story of Ewen Montagu and Operation Mincemeat; the Nazis were fooled into thinking that the initial landings would be through Greece rather than Sicily & Italy. Ep7: The Man Who Hoodwinked Hitler
As the future of Europe hung in the balance, a web of lies tied up German army divisions across Europe with the Allies concealing D-Day plans. S1 E12: D-Day Deceptions
The Free French Forces led by de Gaulle refused to accept the armistice their country had signed with Germany and crossed the Channel to join the fight in Britain. Ep12: The Free French Forces
There is much speculation as to why Jack is taking so long in Scarborough for the divorce from Dolly. Matt finds out Sir Horatio is dealing with the Germans. S3 E4: A Ticket To Care For The Wounded
Arthur is forced to broadcast his first-ever commercial on pirate radio when a rival car dealer tries to put him out of business with a nightmare test drive.
An election threatens Truro's status quo. As his past actions confront him, Ross senses a rival for Demelza's affection. Starring Aidan Turner. S3 E8
Alex thinks she's close to death and has to keep her brain alive by solving a raid at a Post Office; Gene believes the culprit is Chas Cale, a blagger he once crossed. S1 E6
